title: pyrus aucuparia var. lanuginosa
intro: Pyrus aucuparia var. lanuginosa, also known as downy sloe or downy blackthorn, is a variety of the sloe tree (Pyrus aucuparia) native to Europe and Western Asia. This deciduous shrub or small tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pyrus aucuparia var. lanuginosa include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, as well as its use in treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or syrup, and is also used in herbal remedies such as jams, preserves, and teas.
